---
pagetitle: "Ten Tenths — ECE484"
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    page-layout: full
---

::: {.hero-section}

# Ten Tenths {.title}

::: {.subtitle}
Autonomous lap racing on a pre-known floor-line track — no LiDAR, no SLAM.
Pure Pursuit steering · PI velocity control · curvature-based localisation · 1:10 scale F1Tenth
:::

::: {.author-list}

[**Arjun Chainani**](https://example.com),
[**Sam Lee**](https://example.com),
[**Suryansh Singh**](https://example.com)

:::

::: {.affiliation-list}

University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign · ECE 484 · Spring 2026

:::

::: {.button-row}

[[{{< fa video >}} Video]{.btn-text}](#video){.btn .btn-primary}
[[{{< fa brands github >}} Code]{.btn-text}](https://github.com/){.btn .btn-primary}

:::

:::


<!-- ============================================================ -->
<!-- TEASER — System Architecture Block Diagram                   -->
<!-- ============================================================ -->

::: {.section-container}

::: {.hero-teaser}

```{=html}
<svg viewBox="0 0 960 380" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="100%"
     style="max-width:960px;display:block;margin:0 auto;border-radius:10px;box-shadow:0 4px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.08)">
  <defs>
    <marker id="arr" markerWidth="8" markerHeight="6" refX="8" refY="3" orient="auto">
      <polygon points="0 0, 8 3, 0 6" fill="#8fa3c0"/>
    </marker>
    <marker id="arr-blue" markerWidth="8" markerHeight="6" refX="8" refY="3" orient="auto">
      <polygon points="0 0, 8 3, 0 6" fill="#00d4ff"/>
    </marker>
  </defs>

  <!-- background -->
  <rect width="960" height="380" fill="#0a0e17" rx="10"/>

  <!-- OFFLINE column -->
  <rect x="10" y="10" width="160" height="360" rx="6" fill="#141b28" stroke="#2a3a55" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text x="90" y="35" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace" font-size="9" fill="#5a7090" letter-spacing="2">OFFLINE</text>
  <rect x="25" y="48" width="130" height="44" rx="4" fill="#1a2235" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text x="90" y="66" text-anchor="middle" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#e8edf5">Track Geometry</text>
  <text x="90" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace" font-size="9" fill="#8fa3c0">Layout File</text>
  <rect x="25" y="108" width="130" height="44" rx="4" fill="#1a2235" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text x="90" y="126" text-anchor="middle" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#e8edf5">κ(s) Profile</text>
  <text x="90" y="142" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace" font-size="9" fill="#8fa3c0">Curvature Fingerprint</text>
  <rect x="25" y="168" width="130" height="44" rx="4" fill="#1a2235" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text x="90" y="186" text-anchor="middle" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#e8edf5">Racing Line</text>
  <text x="90" y="202" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace" font-size="9" fill="#8fa3c0">Min-Curvature QP</text>
  <rect x="25" y="228" width="130" height="44" rx="4" fill="#1a2235" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text x="90" y="246" text-anchor="middle" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#e8edf5">IPM Homography</text>
  <text x="90" y="262" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace" font-size="9" fill="#8fa3c0">H matrix</text>
  <line x1="90" y1="92" x2="90" y2="108" stroke="#2a3a55" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#arr)"/>
  <line x1="90" y1="152" x2="90" y2="168" stroke="#2a3a55" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#arr)"/>
  <line x1="90" y1="212" x2="90" y2="228" stroke="#2a3a55" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#arr)"/>

  <!-- PERCEPTION column -->
  <rect x="196" y="10" width="160" height="360" rx="6" fill="#141b28" stroke="#2a3a55" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text x="276" y="35" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace" font-size="9" fill="#00d4ff" letter-spacing="2">PERCEPTION</text>
  <rect x="211" y="48" width="130" height="44" rx="4" fill="#1a2235" stroke="#00d4ff" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="276" y="66" text-anchor="middle" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#e8edf5">Stereo Camera</text>
  <text x="276" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace" font-size="9" fill="#00d4ff">D435i / ZED 2</text>
  <rect x="211" y="108" width="130" height="44" rx="4" fill="#1a2235" stroke="#00d4ff" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="276" y="126" text-anchor="middle" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#e8edf5">IPM Warp</text>
  <text x="276" y="142" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace" font-size="9" fill="#00d4ff">Bird's-Eye View</text>
  <rect x="211" y="168" width="130" height="44" rx="4" fill="#1a2235" stroke="#00d4ff" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="276" y="186" text-anchor="middle" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#e8edf5">Lane Segmentation</text>
  <text x="276" y="202" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace" font-size="9" fill="#00d4ff">Adaptive Threshold</text>
  <rect x="211" y="228" width="130" height="44" rx="4" fill="#1a2235" stroke="#00d4ff" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="276" y="246" text-anchor="middle" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#e8edf5">Polynomial Fit</text>
  <text x="276" y="262" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace" font-size="9" fill="#00d4ff">d, ψe, κ_obs</text>
  <line x1="276" y1="92" x2="276" y2="108" stroke="#00d4ff" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="4,3" marker-end="url(#arr-blue)"/>
  <line x1="276" y1="152" x2="276" y2="168" stroke="#00d4ff" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#arr-blue)"/>
  <line x1="276" y1="212" x2="276" y2="228" stroke="#00d4ff" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#arr-blue)"/>
  <line x1="155" y1="250" x2="211" y2="180" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="5,3" marker-end="url(#arr)"/>

  <!-- LOCALISE column -->
  <rect x="382" y="10" width="160" height="360" rx="6" fill="#141b28" stroke="#2a3a55" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text x="462" y="35" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace" font-size="9" fill="#7fff6b" letter-spacing="2">LOCALISE</text>
  <rect x="397" y="48" width="130" height="44" rx="4" fill="#1a2235" stroke="#7fff6b" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="462" y="66" text-anchor="middle" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#e8edf5">Wheel Encoder</text>
  <text x="462" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace" font-size="9" fill="#7fff6b">VESC → v [m/s]</text>
  <rect x="397" y="108" width="130" height="44" rx="4" fill="#1a2235" stroke="#7fff6b" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="462" y="126" text-anchor="middle" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#e8edf5">IMU Gyroscope</text>
  <text x="462" y="142" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace" font-size="9" fill="#7fff6b">Yaw rate ψ̇</text>
  <rect x="397" y="168" width="130" height="44" rx="4" fill="#1a2235" stroke="#7fff6b" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="462" y="186" text-anchor="middle" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#e8edf5">1D Particle Filter</text>
  <text x="462" y="202" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace" font-size="9" fill="#7fff6b">κ_obs vs κ_ref(s) → s</text>
  <rect x="397" y="228" width="130" height="44" rx="4" fill="#1a2235" stroke="#7fff6b" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="462" y="246" text-anchor="middle" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#e8edf5">EKF Fusion</text>
  <text x="462" y="262" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace" font-size="9" fill="#7fff6b">[s, d, ψe, v] @ 200Hz</text>
  <line x1="462" y1="92" x2="462" y2="108" stroke="#7fff6b" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#arr)"/>
  <line x1="462" y1="152" x2="462" y2="168" stroke="#7fff6b" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#arr)"/>
  <line x1="462" y1="212" x2="462" y2="228" stroke="#7fff6b" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#arr)"/>
  <line x1="341" y1="262" x2="397" y2="190" stroke="#00d4ff" stroke-width="1.2" marker-end="url(#arr-blue)"/>
  <line x1="155" y1="130" x2="397" y2="180" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="5,3" marker-end="url(#arr)"/>

  <!-- PLANNING column -->
  <rect x="568" y="10" width="160" height="360" rx="6" fill="#141b28" stroke="#2a3a55" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text x="648" y="35" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace" font-size="9" fill="#ffd166" letter-spacing="2">PLANNING</text>
  <rect x="583" y="48" width="130" height="44" rx="4" fill="#1a2235" stroke="#ffd166" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="648" y="66" text-anchor="middle" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#e8edf5">v_ref Lookup</text>
  <text x="648" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace" font-size="9" fill="#ffd166">v_ref(s) profile</text>
  <rect x="583" y="108" width="130" height="44" rx="4" fill="#1a2235" stroke="#ffd166" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="648" y="126" text-anchor="middle" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#e8edf5">Waypoint Lookup</text>
  <text x="648" y="142" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace" font-size="9" fill="#ffd166">Racing line @ s</text>
  <rect x="583" y="168" width="130" height="44" rx="4" fill="#1a2235" stroke="#ffd166" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="648" y="186" text-anchor="middle" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#e8edf5">Goal Point Select</text>
  <text x="648" y="202" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace" font-size="9" fill="#ffd166">Lookahead L_d(v,κ)</text>
  <line x1="527" y1="250" x2="583" y2="180" stroke="#7fff6b" stroke-width="1.2" marker-end="url(#arr)"/>
  <line x1="155" y1="190" x2="583" y2="115" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="5,3" marker-end="url(#arr)"/>

  <!-- CONTROL column -->
  <rect x="754" y="10" width="192" height="360" rx="6" fill="#141b28" stroke="#2a3a55" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text x="850" y="35" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace" font-size="9" fill="#ff6b35" letter-spacing="2">CONTROL</text>
  <rect x="769" y="48" width="162" height="44" rx="4" fill="#1a2235" stroke="#ff6b35" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="850" y="66" text-anchor="middle" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="600" fill="#ff6b35">Pure Pursuit</text>
  <text x="850" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace" font-size="9" fill="#8fa3c0">δ = atan(2L·sin α / L_d)</text>
  <rect x="769" y="108" width="162" height="44" rx="4" fill="#1a2235" stroke="#ff6b35" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="850" y="126" text-anchor="middle" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="600" fill="#ff6b35">PI Velocity</text>
  <text x="850" y="142" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace" font-size="9" fill="#8fa3c0">a = Kp·e + Ki·∫e dt</text>
  <rect x="769" y="168" width="162" height="44" rx="4" fill="#1a2235" stroke="#ff6b35" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="850" y="186" text-anchor="middle" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#e8edf5">VESC Driver</text>
  <text x="850" y="202" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace" font-size="9" fill="#8fa3c0">ERPM + Servo angle</text>
  <rect x="769" y="228" width="162" height="44" rx="4" fill="#1f2940" stroke="#ff6b35" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="850" y="246" text-anchor="middle" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="600" fill="#e8edf5">Ten Tenths</text>
  <text x="850" y="262" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace" font-size="9" fill="#8fa3c0">Physical Plant</text>
  <line x1="850" y1="92" x2="850" y2="108" stroke="#ff6b35" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#arr)"/>
  <line x1="850" y1="152" x2="850" y2="168" stroke="#ff6b35" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#arr)"/>
  <line x1="850" y1="212" x2="850" y2="228" stroke="#ff6b35" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#arr)"/>
  <line x1="728" y1="140" x2="769" y2="70" stroke="#ffd166" stroke-width="1.2" marker-end="url(#arr)"/>
  <line x1="728" y1="190" x2="769" y2="125" stroke="#ffd166" stroke-width="1.2" marker-end="url(#arr)"/>
  <line x1="341" y1="250" x2="769" y2="70" stroke="#00d4ff" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4,3" marker-end="url(#arr-blue)"/>
</svg>
```

*System architecture — data flows from offline preprocessing (left) through perception, localisation, and planning to the control outputs (right).*

:::

:::


<!-- ============================================================ -->
<!-- ABSTRACT / PROBLEM STATEMENT                                 -->
<!-- ============================================================ -->

::: {.section-container}

## Problem Statement {.section-title}

::: {.abstract-text}
**F1Tenth** is a autonomous driving platform on a 1:10 scale RC car, incorporating IMU, stereo vision, and wheelspeed sensors, and running onboard NVIDIA Jetson compute board.

The competition track uses taped floor lines visual identifiers for the boundaries of the track. The full track geometry (boundaries and centreline) is provided before competition, which makes online mapping redundant.

The system addresses three core objectives:

1. **Lateral localisation** — estimate cross-track error *d* and heading error *ψ_e* in real time from camera images using Inverse Perspective Mapping and polynomial lane fitting.
2. **Longitudinal localisation** — estimate arc-length progress *s* along the track by matching observed lane curvature against a pre-computed curvature profile κ(s) using a 1D particle filter.
3. **Racing line tracking** — follow the minimum-curvature optimised racing line at maximum safe speed using Pure Pursuit lateral control and a PI velocity controller tracking v_ref(s).
:::

:::


<!-- ============================================================ -->
<!-- VIDEO                                                        -->
<!-- ============================================================ -->

::: {.section-container}

## Video {#video .section-title}

::: {.video-container}
<!-- Replace with your YouTube or local video embed -->
{{< video https://youtube.com/shorts/XwaU8WOE3lk?si=EbXz5Lkaug5Z-NAx >}}
:::

:::


<!-- ============================================================ -->
<!-- ALGORITHMS                                                   -->
<!-- ============================================================ -->

::: {.section-container}

## Algorithms {.section-title}

::: {.content-text}

### Inverse Perspective Mapping (Perception)

A homographic warp transforms the perspective camera image into a flat top-down bird's-eye view (BEV). Parallel floor lines that converge at the horizon in the camera frame appear as parallel vertical stripes in BEV — making polynomial lane fitting stable and metric. A 3×3 homography matrix **H** is computed once offline using known floor-plane point correspondences.

```python
BEV_img = cv2.warpPerspective(rect_img, H, (W, H_bev))
# H calibrated using: H, _ = cv2.findHomography(pixel_pts, ground_pts_m)
```

*Ref: Bertozzi & Broggi (1998). GOLD. IEEE Trans. Image Processing 7(1).*

---

### Lane Detection & State Extraction (Perception)

With the processed BEV image, lane pixels are segmented via adaptive thresholding with CLAHE preprocessing. A quadratic polynomial is fitted to each lane boundary; the centreline is the average of the two fits. Three control-relevant quantities are derived at every camera frame:

```python
d     = x_centre(y=0)                         # cross-track error [m]
psi_e = atan2(x_centre'(0), 1)               # heading error [rad]
k_obs = x_centre''(y) / (1+x_centre'**2)**1.5 # observed curvature [1/m]
```

*Ref: Wang et al. (2018). LaneNet. arXiv:1807.01726.*

---

### 1D Particle Filter — Curvature Matching (Localisation)

Arc-length position *s* is estimated by a 1D sequential Monte Carlo filter. Each particle represents a hypothesis about *s*. The **prediction** step propagates particles using wheel velocity; the **update** step weights particles by how well the stored κ_ref(s) matches the observed κ_obs. The track curvature profile κ(s) is a unique geometric fingerprint — each corner narrows the particle distribution.

```python
# Prediction:  s_i ← (s_i + v·dt + N(0, σ_proc)) mod L
# Update:      w_i ← w_i · N(κ_obs; κ_ref(s_i), σ_meas)
# Estimate:    s_hat = Σ w_i · s_i
# σ_proc ≈ 0.02 m,  σ_meas ≈ 0.05 m⁻¹  (tune empirically)
```

*Ref: Thrun, Burgard & Fox (2005). Probabilistic Robotics. MIT Press. Ch. 4.*

---

### Minimum-Curvature Trajectory Optimisation (Planning)

The offline racing line is computed by solving a quadratic program (QP) that minimises the integral of squared curvature along the path, subject to track boundary constraints. This produces the smoothest possible line through the track, reducing required lateral acceleration and allowing higher cornering speeds.

```
min   ∫ κ(s)² ds
s.t.  path stays within left/right boundaries
      v_ref(s) = min(v_max, sqrt(a_lat_max / max(|κ(s)|, ε)))
```

*Ref: Heilmeier et al. (2020). Vehicle System Dynamics 58(10). doi:10.1080/00423114.2019.1631455*

:::

:::


<!-- ============================================================ -->
<!-- CONTROL                                                      -->
<!-- ============================================================ -->

::: {.section-container}

## Control — Pure Pursuit + PI Velocity {.section-title}

::: {.content-text}

### Pure Pursuit (Lateral)

Pure Pursuit is a geometric path-tracking law that steers the car toward a **goal point** on the racing line at a lookahead distance L_d ahead of the vehicle.

**Step 1 — Find closest waypoint.** Using the current *s* estimate from the particle filter, locate the closest racing-line waypoint in O(1) via a pre-sorted arc-length index.

**Step 2 — Select goal point.** Scan forward along the racing line until the Euclidean distance from the car exceeds L_d. This is the goal point (g_x, g_y).

**Step 3 — Compute steering angle.** Transform the goal point into the vehicle's local frame. The angle α between heading and the lookahead vector gives the steering command directly from geometry.

```python
alpha  = atan2(goal_y_local, goal_x_local)  # angle to goal in vehicle frame
kappa  = 2 * sin(alpha) / L_d               # required path curvature
delta  = atan(kappa * L_wb)                 # Ackermann steering angle [rad]
```

**Adaptive lookahead** — L_d scales with speed and track curvature:

| Condition | L_d | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| v < 2 m/s | 0.5 m (minimum) | Tight tracking at low speed |
| v = 4 m/s | 1.5 – 2.0 m | Balanced stability/accuracy |
| v = 7 m/s | 2.5 – 3.5 m | Smooth high-speed line |
| High \|κ(s)\| | Reduce by 20–30% | Tighter corner tracking |
| Straight \|κ\|≈0 | Increase to v · k_gain | Maximum straight speed |

*Rule: `L_d = clip(k · v, L_min, L_max) + κ_correction`*
*Ref: Coulter (1992). CMU-RI-TR-92-01. / Sukhil & Behl (2021). arXiv:2111.08873.*

---

### PI Velocity Controller (Longitudinal)

A PI controller tracks v_ref(s) — the target speed at the current arc-length position, looked up from the offline racing-line velocity profile. The integral term eliminates steady-state speed error. Anti-windup clamping prevents integrator overshoot during braking.

```python
e_v   = v_ref(s_est) - v_measured                 # speed error [m/s]
I_v   = clip(I_v + Ki * e_v * dt, -I_max, I_max)  # anti-windup
a_cmd = Kp * e_v + I_v                            # throttle/brake command
# Send a_cmd to VESC as target ERPM via vesc_driver
```

- **Kp** — sets acceleration aggressiveness toward v_ref. Start at Kp ≈ 0.5, increase until slight overshoot, then reduce 20%.
- **Ki** — eliminates steady-state speed error under friction load. Recommended Ki ≈ 0.1–0.3. Always pair with anti-windup clamping.
- **v_ref source** — look up from the *closest waypoint* to the car, not the lookahead point. Using the lookahead point's speed causes premature braking before corners.

:::

:::


<!-- ============================================================ -->
<!-- RESULTS / PROGRESS                                          -->
<!-- ============================================================ -->

::: {.section-container}

## Results {.section-title}

::: {.content-text}
The system is currently under active development and hardware integration. The simulator baseline achieves stable lane-following laps at 3 m/s using Pure Pursuit with fixed lookahead. Below is the current software progress across all subsystems.
:::

```{=html}
<div style="max-width:600px;margin:1.5rem auto 0;">
  <div style="margin-bottom:12px;">
    <div style="display:flex;justify-content:space-between;font-size:0.9rem;color:#555;margin-bottom:4px;"><span>IPM Lane Detection</span><span>85%</span></div>
    <div style="height:6px;background:#e0e0e0;border-radius:3px;overflow:hidden;"><div style="height:100%;width:85%;background:#4a6cf7;border-radius:3px;"></div></div>
  </div>
  <div style="margin-bottom:12px;">
    <div style="display:flex;justify-content:space-between;font-size:0.9rem;color:#555;margin-bottom:4px;"><span>Pure Pursuit (simulator)</span><span>75%</span></div>
    <div style="height:6px;background:#e0e0e0;border-radius:3px;overflow:hidden;"><div style="height:100%;width:75%;background:#4a6cf7;border-radius:3px;"></div></div>
  </div>
  <div style="margin-bottom:12px;">
    <div style="display:flex;justify-content:space-between;font-size:0.9rem;color:#555;margin-bottom:4px;"><span>PI Velocity Controller</span><span>60%</span></div>
    <div style="height:6px;background:#e0e0e0;border-radius:3px;overflow:hidden;"><div style="height:100%;width:60%;background:#4a6cf7;border-radius:3px;"></div></div>
  </div>
  <div style="margin-bottom:12px;">
    <div style="display:flex;justify-content:space-between;font-size:0.9rem;color:#555;margin-bottom:4px;"><span>1D Particle Filter</span><span>40%</span></div>
    <div style="height:6px;background:#e0e0e0;border-radius:3px;overflow:hidden;"><div style="height:100%;width:40%;background:#4a6cf7;border-radius:3px;"></div></div>
  </div>
  <div style="margin-bottom:12px;">
    <div style="display:flex;justify-content:space-between;font-size:0.9rem;color:#555;margin-bottom:4px;"><span>Hardware Integration</span><span>15%</span></div>
    <div style="height:6px;background:#e0e0e0;border-radius:3px;overflow:hidden;"><div style="height:100%;width:15%;background:#4a6cf7;border-radius:3px;"></div></div>
  </div>
</div>
```

:::


<!-- ============================================================ -->
<!-- KEY CHALLENGES                                               -->
<!-- ============================================================ -->

::: {.section-container}

## Key Challenges & Mitigations {.section-title}

::: {.content-text}

**Long Straight — Curvature Ambiguity.**
κ≈0 on straights gives no localisation signal. The particle filter relies on velocity propagation to maintain *s* during straights. The IPM BEV sees 1–3 m ahead, so corner entry curvature is visible before the car reaches it.

**Pure Pursuit Corner Cutting.**
Long L_d causes the car to cut apexes. Reduce L_d proportionally to |κ(s)| at high-curvature waypoints. The pre-known curvature profile enables a feedforward schedule — L_d is reduced *before* entering each corner, not reactively.

**IPM Lane Detection Failure.**
Shadows, reflections, or line damage degrade detection. CLAHE preprocessing normalises lighting. Fallback: if polynomial fit residual exceeds threshold for >5 frames, reduce speed to 50% and hold last-valid lane estimate. CNN-based segmentation (LaneNet TensorRT) as a drop-in upgrade.

**PI Integrator Windup.**
Integrator state lags during hard braking. Clamp to ±I_max. Reset integrator when sign of velocity error changes (conditional integration). VESC internal PID speed mode handles low-level torque management.

**Motion Blur at Race Speed.**
High speed causes camera exposure blur. Reduce exposure time (increase gain to compensate brightness). Use IR channel of RealSense D435i — floor lines have higher contrast under IR. GPU-accelerate rectification via `isaac_ros_image_proc` on Jetson to maintain 60 fps.

**Particle Filter Convergence Time.**
*s* is unknown at race start — 1–3 lap convergence required. Start Phase 1 (Lane PID, no *s* needed) at reduced speed until particle filter ESS > N/2. The filter typically converges within the first 2–3 corners (~5 seconds). Transition to full racing speed automatically when convergence is confirmed.

:::

:::


<!-- ============================================================ -->
<!-- TEAM & PLAN                                                  -->
<!-- ============================================================ -->

::: {.section-container}

## Team & Project Plan {.section-title}

::: {.content-text}

**Arjun Chainani** — *Perception Lead.*
IPM homography calibration, lane segmentation, polynomial fitting pipeline, ROS 2 perception node.

**Sam Lee** — *Localisation Lead.*
1D particle filter implementation, curvature profile computation, EKF integration, convergence analysis.

**Suryansh Singh** — *Control & Planning Lead.*
Pure Pursuit implementation, adaptive lookahead tuning, PI velocity controller, VESC integration, offline trajectory optimisation.

:::

::: {.content-text}

**Project Timeline**

| Period | Milestone | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Week 1–2 · Jan 2026 | Research & Architecture | ✅ Done |
| Week 3–4 · Jan–Feb 2026 | Simulator Setup & IPM Pipeline | ✅ Done |
| Week 5–6 · Feb–Mar 2026 | Particle Filter + Pure Pursuit | 🔄 In Progress |
| Week 7–8 · Mar 2026 | Hardware Deployment | 📋 Planned |
| Week 9–10 · Apr 2026 | Racing Line + Speed Optimisation | 📋 Planned |
| Week 11–12 · Apr–May 2026 | Competition Preparation | 📋 Planned |

:::

:::


<!-- ============================================================ -->
<!-- RELATED WORK / REFERENCES                                    -->
<!-- ============================================================ -->

::: {.section-container}

## References {.section-title}

::: {.content-text}

[1] Coulter, R.C. (1992). *Implementation of the Pure Pursuit Path Tracking Algorithm.* CMU-RI-TR-92-01. Carnegie Mellon Robotics Institute.

[2] Sukhil, V. & Behl, M. (2021). *Adaptive Lookahead Pure-Pursuit for Autonomous Racing.* arXiv:2111.08873. 20% improvement in lap time over fixed-lookahead baseline on F1Tenth.

[3] Heilmeier, A. et al. (2020). *Minimum Curvature Trajectory Planning and Control for an Autonomous Race Car.* Vehicle System Dynamics 58(10). doi:10.1080/00423114.2019.1631455

[4] O'Kelly, M. et al. (2019). *F1/10: An Open-Source Autonomous Cyber-Physical Platform.* arXiv:1901.08567.

[5] Bertozzi, M. & Broggi, A. (1998). *GOLD: A Parallel Real-Time Stereo Vision System for Generic Obstacle and Lane Detection.* IEEE Trans. Image Processing 7(1). doi:10.1109/83.650851

[6] Thrun, S., Burgard, W. & Fox, D. (2005). *Probabilistic Robotics.* MIT Press. Ch. 4: Nonparametric Filters.

[7] Moore, T. & Stouch, D. (2014). *A Generalized Extended Kalman Filter Implementation for ROS.* Intelligent Autonomous Systems 13.

[8] Snider, J.M. (2009). *Automatic Steering Methods for Autonomous Automobile Path Tracking.* CMU-RI-TR-09-08.

[9] Farkas, T. et al. (2025). *Integrating a Stereo Vision System on the F1Tenth Platform.* MDPI Engineering Proceedings.

[10] Elgouhary, M. & El-Wakeel, A.S. (2026). *Learning to Tune Pure Pursuit in Autonomous Racing: Joint Lookahead and Steering-Gain Control with PPO.* arXiv:2602.18386.

[11] Evans, B. et al. (2024). *Unifying F1Tenth Autonomous Racing: Survey, Methods and Benchmarks.* arXiv:2402.18558.

[12] Raji, A. et al. (2023). *Model- and Acceleration-Based Pursuit Controller for High-Performance Autonomous Racing.* arXiv:2209.04346.

:::

:::


<!-- ============================================================ -->
<!-- BIBTEX                                                       -->
<!-- ============================================================ -->

::: {.section-container}

## BibTeX {.section-title}

```bibtex
@techreport{tentenths2026,
  author      = {Chainani, Arjun and Lee, Sam and Singh, Suryansh},
  title       = {Ten Tenths: Vision-Only Autonomous Lap Racing on a Known-Track F1Tenth Platform},
  institution = {University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign},
  year        = {2026},
  note        = {ECE 484 Project Report, Spring 2026},
}
```

:::


<!-- ============================================================ -->
<!-- FOOTER                                                       -->
<!-- ============================================================ -->

::: {.site-footer}

This website template is adapted from the
[Nerfies](https://nerfies.github.io) project page, which is licensed under a
[Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International License](http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/).

:::
